name: Sydney
localTips: Get an Opal card, or just tap a contactless bank card or phone directly, for trains, buses and ferries — it automatically caps your daily and weekly spend.
Sydney sun is fierce even on cloudy days — the UV index regularly hits extreme, so slip on a shirt, slop on sunscreen and slap on a hat.
Surf beaches are patrolled between the red-and-yellow flags only — swim outside them and you're on your own with rips that catch even strong swimmers.
Tipping isn't expected — service charges aren't added and staff are paid a living wage, so a tip is a bonus rather than an obligation.
Trains, buses and ferries thin out after midnight on weeknights — Friday and Saturday keep NightRide buses running when the trains stop.

activities.title: Sydney Opera House exterior & steps
activities.description: Walk the sail-shaped shells up close and sit on the box-office steps facing the harbour — free, and arguably the best photo angle in the whole city.

activities.title: Circular Quay waterfront
activities.description: Ferries, buskers and the Harbour Bridge framing the Opera House — Sydney's transport hub doubles as its best free harbour view, especially at sunset.

activities.title: Royal Botanic Garden
activities.description: 30 hectares of harbourside gardens between the Opera House and Mrs Macquarie's Point — free entry, fruit bats in the palm grove, and a view back at the CBD skyline.

activities.title: Bondi to Coogee coastal walk
activities.description: A 6km clifftop path past Tamarama, Bronte and Clovelly's ocean pools — one of the world's great free urban coastal walks, with a swim stop wherever you like.

activities.title: Bondi Beach
activities.description: Australia's most famous stretch of sand — surfers, the Icebergs ocean pool at the southern end, and a Saturday farmers' market at the surf club.

activities.title: Darling Harbour
activities.description: A waterfront precinct of restaurants, fountains and free public events — Tumbalong Park hosts festivals and a nightly light show at the pyrmont bridge on weekends.

activities.title: The Rocks historic streets
activities.description: Cobblestone lanes of Sydney's oldest colonial quarter, wedged under the Harbour Bridge — sandstone warehouses turned into galleries, pubs and a weekend market.

activities.title: Sydney Harbour Bridge pedestrian walkway
activities.description: Walk across the 'Coathanger' on the free eastern footpath for harbour views from Milsons Point to the Rocks — no ticket needed, unlike the summit climb.

activities.title: Manly Beach & ferry crossing
activities.description: The Manly ferry's 30-minute harbour crossing is one of the world's best commutes for the price of a bus fare — arrive to a proper surf beach and the Corso's food strip.

activities.title: Barangaroo Reserve
activities.description: A reclaimed headland park built from 75,000 native plants and sandstone salvaged from the site's harbour excavation — one of the CBD's newest green spaces.

activities.title: Centennial Park
activities.description: 189 hectares of ponds, fig-tree avenues and horse-riding trails — locals jog, cycle and picnic here on weekends, with flying-fox colonies overhead at dusk.

activities.title: Paddington Markets
activities.description: A Saturday-only market in a church courtyard since 1973 — indie designers, vintage stalls and buskers along one of Sydney's best-preserved Victorian terrace streets.

activities.title: Watsons Bay & The Gap
activities.description: A harbourside village that ends at The Gap, a dramatic ocean clifftop lookout — fish and chips at Doyles on the wharf finishes the ferry trip properly.

activities.title: Mrs Macquarie's Chair
activities.description: A sandstone bench hand-carved into the rock in 1810 for the governor's wife — still the postcard angle on the Opera House and Harbour Bridge together in one frame.

activities.title: Queen Victoria Building arcade
activities.description: An 1898 Romanesque shopping palace with stained-glass domes and a hand-carved royal clock — free to wander regardless of whether you buy anything.

activities.title: State Library of NSW reading room
activities.description: A grand Art Deco reading room with a stained-glass zodiac ceiling and free rotating exhibitions on Australian history — open to anyone, no library card required to browse.

activities.title: Newtown King Street alt scene
activities.description: Terrace houses painted in murals, secondhand bookshops and the city's densest run of vegan cafes and dive bars along King Street's kilometre-long strip.
activities.insiderNote: Duck down the side streets off King Street for the best mural work — the laneway behind the Camperdown end has some of the most photographed pieces.

activities.title: Spice Alley hawker-style laneway
activities.description: A faux back-alley of communal benches and open kitchens tucked behind Central Park mall — Malaysian, Vietnamese and Sichuan stalls at hawker-centre prices.
activities.insiderNote: It's cash- and card-friendly with no cover, but arrives busy at lunch — a 2pm visit gets you the same food with no queue.

activities.title: Hidden rooftop bar
activities.description: Unmarked rooftop bars above CBD laneways — like the tucked-away entrance to Frankie's Pizza's upstairs room — trade the view for a speakeasy vibe without a hotel markup.
activities.insiderNote: Look for a plain door and a buzzer beside an otherwise ordinary shopfront — most of Sydney's best small bars deliberately have no street signage.

activities.title: Sydney Fish Market early morning
activities.description: The southern hemisphere's largest fish market runs a public auction viewing before dawn — grab freshly shucked oysters at the sushi counters once trading opens.
activities.insiderNote: The public gallery for the Dutch-clock auction opens around 5:30am — come for the auction, stay for breakfast sashimi at the market's own sushi bars.

activities.title: Small bar laneway hopping
activities.description: A 2008 licensing law reform let tiny 60-person bars open in CBD laneways — Angel Place and Ash Street cluster half a dozen within a five-minute walk.
activities.insiderNote: Small bars close their doors once at capacity rather than turning people away at a rope line — arrive before 9pm on weekends to guarantee entry.

activities.title: Freshwater Beach — the locals' surf spot
activities.description: A short walk from Manly but nowhere near as crowded, Freshwater is where Duke Kahanamoku first demonstrated surfing in Australia in 1915 — a plaque marks the spot.
activities.insiderNote: Locals surf the northern end near the rock pool; the southern end by the surf club is calmer and better for a first-timer's swim.

activities.title: Local pub with live music
activities.description: Inner-west pubs like the Marrickville Bowling Club or the Sando still book local bands most nights of the week — cheap beer, no cover, none of the Kings Cross tourist markup.
activities.insiderNote: Check the venue's Instagram the day of — inner-west pub gig lineups change constantly and rarely make it onto ticketing sites.

activities.title: Surry Hills laneway café culture
activities.description: Sydney's specialty-coffee scene concentrates in Surry Hills' converted warehouses and terrace fronts — Single O and Reuben Hills started the wave that's now citywide.
activities.insiderNote: Skip the queue at the famous names and try the unmarked cafes on Foveaux and Bourke Streets — the baristas trained at the same roasteries.

activities.title: Sydney Opera House tour or performance
activities.description: A backstage guided tour into the concert hall and green rooms, or better, book an actual performance — even a matinee puts you inside Utzon's sail-shaped acoustics.
activities.bookingQuery: Sydney Opera House guided tour ticket

activities.title: Sydney Harbour BridgeClimb
activities.description: Clip into a safety line and climb the outer arch of the Harbour Bridge to the summit, 134 metres above the water — dawn and twilight climbs sell out first.

activities.title: Taronga Zoo
activities.description: A hillside zoo with arguably the best backdrop of any in the world — giraffes framed by the Opera House and skyline across the harbour, reached by its own ferry.

activities.title: Blue Mountains day trip
activities.description: The Three Sisters rock formation, the Scenic World cable car over an ancient rainforest gorge, and eucalyptus-hazed valley views 90 minutes from the city.
activities.bookingQuery: Blue Mountains day trip from Sydney

activities.title: Sydney Harbour dinner cruise
activities.description: A covered cruise boat loops past the Opera House and under the Harbour Bridge with a three-course dinner — best booked for the sunset-into-lights-up departure.

activities.title: Bondi Beach surf lesson
activities.description: Small-group lessons on the sand at the north end of Bondi, board and wetsuit included — most people stand up by the end of a two-hour session.

activities.title: Whale watching cruise
activities.description: Humpback whales migrate past Sydney Heads twice a year — a small boat gets you close to breaching pods on their way north in July or south with calves in October.

activities.title: Sydney Tower Eye observation deck
activities.description: A 360-degree deck 250 metres above the CBD — on a clear day the view stretches from the Blue Mountains to the Pacific horizon; add the 4D cinema for a full ticket.
activities.bookingQuery: Sydney Tower Eye observation deck ticket

activities.title: SEA LIFE Sydney Aquarium
activities.description: Walk-through tunnels beneath sharks, rays and a Great Barrier Reef tank at Darling Harbour — a solid rainy-day option that still feels like a proper attraction.

activities.title: Jet boat harbour ride
activities.description: 270-degree spins and high-speed runs past the Opera House and Harbour Bridge — a loud, soaking, thrill-ride version of the standard ferry commute.
